title: Ranunculus neglectus
taxonomy: Ranunculus neglectus is classified within the family Ranunculaceae, commonly known as the buttercup family. This family consists of approximately 1,900 species of flowering plants. Ranunculus neglectus is further classified as belonging to the genus Ranunculus, which includes around 600 species of plants. The genus Ranunculus is often divided into several sections, with Ranunculus neglectus belonging to the section Ranunculus. Ranunculus neglectus is native to North America, specifically the western United States and southwestern Canada.
uses: Ranunculus neglectus, also known as the forget-me-not, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine. Its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are believed to help alleviate skin irritations and wounds. The plant's leaves and flowers are often used to treat various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. Additionally, it is believed to have antimicrobial properties, which can help protect against infections. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential benefits.
benefits: Ranunculus neglectus, also known as the small-flowered buttercup, has been traditionally used in folk medicine. Its roots are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate pain and swelling. The plant is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, which could aid in wound healing and preventing infection. Ranunculus neglectus may also possess antioxidant properties, which could help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals. However, more research is needed to confirm the full extent of its health benefits.

The plant's extracts have been found to exhibit antioxidant activity, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. constituents: I can find information on this aspect of the plant. Ranunculus neglectus contains ranunculin, a toxic compound that can be released when the plant is damaged or bruised. This compound can cause skin irritation and other adverse effects in humans and animals. Other active constituents of Ranunculus neglectus may include saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, although more research is needed to confirm their presence and effects. side_effects: Ranunculus neglectus, also known as the wood buttercup, is known to cause skin irritation and blistering if the plant's sap comes into contact with human skin. The sap of the plant contains a toxic compound called ranunculin, which can cause severe skin burns and blisters. Ingestion of the plant's parts can also lead to oral and gastrointestinal irritation, as well as other systemic effects. The plant's toxic compounds can cause allergic reactions, and repeated exposure may lead to increased sensitivity. It's essential to handle the plant with caution and avoid ingesting any parts of the plant to avoid potential side effects.
intro: Ranunculus neglectus, also known as Buttercup, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and shiny, green leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, providing relief from arthritis, gout, and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Ranunculus neglectus include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Ranunculus neglectus is commonly used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
